text:
There once was a man named Oliver who lived in a small village. One day Oliver went on a journey into the forests. As he walked, he heard laughter and saw little glowing mice. They told him a secret; the moon was a giant owl!

Filled with curiosity, Oliver followed moonlit glints.

At night, he found the owl perched on a tree. It didn't speak, but winked every time he dared to look closer.

Every night after that, Oliver visited the owl.

The owl taught him magic moonbeam spells.

One windy night, Oliver said, "Moonbeam bloom!"

Flowers everywhere burst in sparkling moonlight.

The owl winked proudly.

Every night for a week, Oliver practiced.

One quiet dawn, with an owl-cry like laughter, he vanished.

The villagers didn't know he'd seen the moon owl.

But every night after, they'd hear singing.

And Oliver lived happily ever after, weaving spells with moonlight.
